# Transcrição: 3-Targeting - How We Get Started

## [00:00 - 03:01]

We may have spoken about this before, but it's important that we recap on this when it comes to targeting your customers. So when you're working with YouTube advertising, you're actually using the Google Ads platform. It's been around for many years. It's a very, very powerful platform, and we have so many different ways of targeting our customers. With this in mind, it can feel quite complex and complicated, so I'm going to simplify it and show you where we should get started from the very beginning. So as a quick overview, you have things like affinity audiences, which are audiences based on interests. So if you're really into surfing, for example, the chance to go to lots of surfing websites, YouTube and Google are tracking that about you, and as a result, you would fall into an affinity audience that Google have already predefined of surfers, for example. And then if you had a product you wanted to show to surfers, you could choose that audience. Now, these are predefined, as I say, from Google. You can build your own, which are called customer affinity audiences, and you can choose things like keywords, websites, apps on people's phones and places that people check into that mean that Google can have all this information to create your own custom affinity audiences that you can use. But we're going to target these types of audiences at a much, much later stage. These are big audiences, tough to crack, but when you do, it means you can really scale. You also have similar audiences, which is similar to lookalike audiences if you're familiar with YouTube or with Facebook advertising, sorry. So similar audiences are audiences that Google and YouTube will build off of existing data. So you can build similar audiences off of your current email lists, your website converters, your website visitors, all that type of thing. YouTube and Google will build similar audiences normally, actually, automatically for you. Then you'll have in-market audiences. These are people that have recently changed their behavior online in the last seven to fourteen days because they're starting to do new types of search they don't often do. Now, that might be something like shopping for a TV and you're doing lots of research about which TVs are best. Not something you would do on a regular basis like you're, like, a massive interest in it, but you've recently upped your interest, so to speak, in a particular subject matter like TVs or if you're going on holiday, the chances are you've been looking up things like car rental, hotels, flights, that type of thing. Google are tracking everything about you and that means that you're falling into certain in-market audiences based on that recent change in behavior. You also have topics, so this will be all the videos on YouTube will be categorized into topics. Now, this is not like the categories that the YouTube creators would choose, like education or sports or gaming, for example.

## [03:01 - 06:03]

Topic targeting when it comes to Google Ads is much more detailed and there'll be so many different topics out there that you can select from. These are predefined from Google and you don't have an option to create your own custom topic, but if there are topics out there that are relevant, it's always good to try those out as well. Now, you can see that we're going from big, big audiences to starting to get into smaller audiences, but more focused. So in-market audience would be a smaller audience to an affinity audience, typically, but it's people are much more likely to be interested because they're in the market for that particular product or service at that time. And if they're looking up topics or they're watching videos about certain topics and we know that we're getting closer and closer, it's being really zeroed in and the targeting becomes tighter. Now, custom intent is a fantastic new targeting option. It's not something that you can get to start doing immediately from day one, but it's something we'll move into very quickly indeed. So custom intent targeting is where you build your own audiences based off people's keywords they're typing in. That could be Google data and YouTube data. And it's also based on people's behaviors online, based on the videos that might be watching or websites that might be clicking on. And it's that kind of custom intent audience that's built off kind of search, but it's an audience of people that are looking up that type of stuff. So it means we can target an audience based on their recent search behavior, which is really exciting and works incredibly well as well. Now, you also have keyword targeting, which is inside someone's session, which is normally like 60 to maybe 30 to 60 minutes or so. During a session, duration on YouTube, we're focused on YouTube for the time being, of course, if they've typed in a keyword recently in that session, the last 30 to 60 minutes, there are full-interact keyword category, which means we can run our ads in front of people that have recently typed that in based on Google or YouTube data. So even though they might be not watching a video that's entirely relevant to our keyword at that time, we still know they recently typed in that keyword, and it's useful to show the ad to that person because it's something they've typed in in that session. And you also have placement targeting. This is where someone's actually watching a very specific video that you've selected at that moment in time. So you're saying, I want to get my ad in front of that video there, and you literally select the URL of the video you want to run your ad in front of. And that is a phenomenal way to target. It doesn't give you that much scale, but it does give you incredible ROI typically because you're being so granular, you can focus your efforts and your time on keyword or on placement, sorry, that are likely to get you very, very good results. So it's a great way of getting started. And then, of course, you have websites and video remarketing as well. So basically people go into your website engaging or watching your videos. You can get back in front of them with other video ads, should you wish.

## [06:03 - 06:59]

Now, when we first get started, I want to make sure you can really nail the cold search audiences. And the two areas I want to make sure we really focus on is keyword targeting and placement targeting. Now, placement is a lot easier to get very, very, very good results. But as I said before, it's very difficult to get to the scale there. But it's a great place to get started. And also keywords can be fantastic, not only for becoming a little bit more broader, so you can target more people. So it really does scale at this point. But also it's still very relevant. So keywords are slightly harder to crack than placement targeting. So we're going to start with placement targeting and move into keyword targeting. So with that in mind, placement targeting, we're literally going to select the individual videos we want to run our ad in front of. I'm going to show you how to start doing that in the very next video. I'll see you in the next video.
